behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are typically wiring/mapping bugs between orchestrator and reviewer adapters. You need real module wiring (and usually request-capture at adapter boundaries), but not live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with a real contract fixture and real reviewer adapter modules replaced only with capture doubles at the transport boundary. Assert all three invocations receive identical document payload bytes and each role receives its own instruction pack (extractor/risk/compliance).

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on actual model interpretation/extraction quality, including variability in phrasing and model nondeterminism.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the production clause-extractor model path on a real contract containing known parties, dates, obligations, and penalties; fail if required terms are missed or mislabeled beyond acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is parameter propagation/serialization correctness; real model output is not required to detect config-passing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor invocation through real orchestration and adapter code with a concrete risk policy fixture; capture outbound request and assert policy fields/version/thresholds are preserved exactly.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Detecting indirect/euphemistic risk language is semantic and model-dependent; stubs cannot reveal real failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Run the production risk-assessor model on contract passages with explicit and euphemistic risky phrasing under a real policy config; assert flagged findings include those passages with appropriate severity.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation-shape correctness (context propagation) and is best caught with real module wiring and request inspection.
behaviors.test_description: Call compliance-checker invocation through real orchestration using a fixture with specific jurisdiction and regulatory profile; inspect outbound payload and verify exact jurisdiction code/profile IDs passed through.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correct compliance determination over legal language usually depends on model reasoning and can vary across runs/models.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the production compliance checker on contracts with known expected compliance outcomes for a configured regulatory profile; fail when required controls are incorrectly marked pass/fail.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Conflict-preserving merge logic can be validated as a pure in-memory transformation; no I/O or live services are required.
behaviors.test_description: Pass in-memory reviewer outputs containing the same clause ID with conflicting labels (e.g., extractor=standard, risk=high risk) to the merge function; assert merged report contains both assessments and reviewer attribution metadata unchanged.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This requires the full multi-step pipeline with three parallel agentic reviewer calls plus merge; failures can emerge only across step interactions and sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full production pipeline on a real contract: parallel dispatch to live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers, collect all outputs, execute merge, and assert every finding from each reviewer is present in the final unified report with no dropped items.
